positioning: Instant land records and title search, digitally
description: Landeed lets buyers, lawyers, and developers instantly pull land records, encumbrance certificates, and title documents from government portals across India — without visiting sub-registrar offices. Available for Telangana, AP, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and expanding.
key_problem: Land title search in India requires physical visits to sub-registrar offices; documents are in regional languages and hard to interpret. Landeed digitises access and provides plain-English summaries.
revenue_model: Per-report fees (₹99–₹999 per document pull), monthly API subscription for law firms and banks
differentiator: API-first; only player with direct government portal integration for instant EC pulls
weakness: Coverage limited to ~5 states; no soil/environment or demand analytics layer
notes: YC alumni. Closest direct competitor to KYL on the title-search layer. Does not do holistic land reports.

positioning: India's largest real estate data analytics platform
description: PropEquity aggregates RERA, IGRS, and listing data across 45+ cities to provide demand analytics, price trends, project completion risk, and competitive intelligence. Used by developers, banks, and PE funds.
key_problem: Developers and investors making multi-crore decisions with incomplete market data.
revenue_model: Annual SaaS subscriptions (₹5L–₹25L/yr). Custom research reports.
differentiator: Deepest RERA data integration; 15+ years of historical price data
weakness: No title/legal layer; no physical land attributes. Expensive for individuals.
notes: Bootstrapped and profitable. Competes on demand analytics and market sizing.

positioning: India's oldest independent real estate research firm
description: Liases Foras provides quarterly residential market reports covering 42 cities — unit launches, absorption rates, price indices, unsold inventory. Primary data collector for MoHUA's national housing statistics.
key_problem: No credible, independent source for India housing market data free from developer PR bias.
revenue_model: Subscription reports, custom research for government and NBFC clients, media licensing
differentiator: Trusted by MoHUA, NHB, and Supreme Court committees. Oldest and most cited.
weakness: PDF-report model. No tech platform. No land-specific focus.
notes: Research firm, not a product. Competes on market research reports for developers/investors.

positioning: Registered sale deed data for transparent property valuations
description: Zapkey pulls registered sale transactions from IGR Maharashtra and makes them searchable — enabling buyers to see actual transaction prices (not asking prices) in any building. Covers 30+ cities.
key_problem: Indian real estate is opaque — asking prices are inflated. Zapkey shows real sold prices from government records.
revenue_model: Freemium — basic search free, detailed reports ₹499. B2B API for banks and valuers.
differentiator: Building-level registered transaction history in consumer-friendly UI
weakness: Residential apartment focus; no land/plot layer, no title search
notes: Narrower focus. Competes on price discovery but not due diligence.

positioning: AI-powered property search with verified listings for NCR
description: Clicbrics uses AI to match buyers with properties, offers virtual tours, and provides a curated, verified listing experience. Known for Delhi NCR market focus and machine-learning-based recommendation engine. App available on Play Store and App Store.
key_problem: Property search on major portals is cluttered with fake listings and unresponsive agents. Clicbrics filters and verifies.
revenue_model: Developer advertising, brokerage commission on closed transactions
differentiator: AI recommendation + verified listings; strong video/virtual tour library
weakness: NCR-centric; no data/analytics product; no land due diligence
notes: Adjacent portal. No overlap with KYL's data/due diligence layer.

positioning: Residential property listings portal, part of REA India group
description: Makaan.com is a residential listings portal under the REA India umbrella (alongside Housing.com and PropTiger). Serves buyers and renters with listings across 600+ Indian cities. App on Play Store and App Store with 1M+ downloads.
key_problem: Discovery of verified residential properties across India for buyers and renters.
revenue_model: Developer listing fees, agent subscription plans
differentiator: REA Group data + Housing.com cross-listing synergies
weakness: No standalone product differentiation from Housing.com; no analytics/data product
notes: Portal competitor, not a data or due diligence product.

positioning: South India focused property search portal backed by The Hindu Group
description: RoofandFloor is a property listings portal with a strong South India (Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Telangana, AP) focus. Backed by The Hindu Group — gives it editorial credibility and regional language reach. App on Play Store.
key_problem: North-India-centric portals (99acres, Magicbricks) miss South India nuance — languages, local builders, local area names.
revenue_model: Builder advertising, agent subscriptions
differentiator: The Hindu brand trust + regional language listings + South India builder relationships
weakness: Limited pan-India scale; no analytics product; no due diligence
notes: Niche geographic play. No direct overlap with KYL.

positioning: Online property legal documents, title opinion, and registration assistance
description: LegalDesk provides online legal document drafting (sale deeds, NOCs, property agreements) and connects buyers with lawyers for title opinion and due diligence. Offers end-to-end property registration facilitation in select cities.
key_problem: Property legal documentation in India requires physical visits to lawyers and notaries.
revenue_model: Per-document fees (₹500–₹5,000), fixed-fee legal packages (₹15,000–₹50,000), lawyer marketplace commission
differentiator: Lawyer marketplace + document automation; registered with Bar Council
weakness: Human-powered title search (slow, inconsistent quality). No analytics layer.
notes: Adjacent on due diligence — legal-services model vs. data-product model.

positioning: AI-powered digitisation of land records from physical documents
description: Emerging player using OCR and ML to digitise land revenue records (RoR, patta, khata) from physical documents, helping state governments and NGOs create digital cadastral maps. Pilots in Karnataka and Madhya Pradesh.
key_problem: Over 60% of India's land records still exist only in physical form — handwritten in regional languages across taluk offices.
revenue_model: Government contract (SaaS + digitisation fee per village). Early-stage B2B API.
differentiator: OCR trained on Indian regional-language land documents (Kannada, Hindi)
weakness: Very early stage. Government procurement cycle is slow. No consumer product.
notes: Infrastructure player — could become partner or competitor.

positioning: Property verification as add-on to NoBroker's platform
description: NoBroker offers property verification (physical site visit, ownership documents check, encumbrance check) delivered within 5–7 days. Priced at ₹3,500–₹7,500 per property.
key_problem: Buyers face risk of fake listings and undisclosed encumbrances on NoBroker's platform.
revenue_model: One-time flat fee per property verification (₹3,500–₹7,500)
differentiator: Embedded in transaction flow; NoBroker's 10M+ user base
weakness: Human-powered, not scalable; apartment-centric; primarily rental market
notes: Not a standalone land research product. Apartment-centric.

positioning: Central registry for securitisation assets — India's only national lien database
description: CERSAI is the GOI-mandated registry where banks must register mortgages and security interests over immovable property. Any lender financing a property must search CERSAI first. Handles 4M+ registrations per year.
key_problem: Multiple banks financing the same property ('double-financing fraud') was a major NPA risk.
revenue_model: Government entity — fee per search (₹50–₹500) and registration (₹250–₹500). Revenue ~₹80 Cr/yr.
differentiator: Mandated by SARFAESI Act. Only authoritative source for national mortgage/lien data.
weakness: Paid search only, no API for bulk access. Only covers registered mortgages.
notes: Not a competitor — a critical data source KYL should integrate.

positioning: America's #1 real estate marketplace with AI-powered home valuations (Zestimate)
description: Zillow is the largest US real estate portal with 230M+ monthly visits. Famous for Zestimate — an AI valuation model for every US home. Also operates Zillow Offers (iBuying), Zillow Home Loans, and ShowingTime. Listed on NASDAQ.
key_problem: Home buyers in the US had no easy way to discover, value, and transact on homes without a broker.
revenue_model: Agent advertising (Premier Agent), mortgage origination fees, rentals listing fees. Revenue: ~$1.9B (FY2024)
differentiator: Zestimate AI valuation + largest US consumer real estate brand + agent advertising network
weakness: US-only. Zestimate failed for unique/rural land (led to iBuying losses of $500M+). No land/plot focus.
notes: Best consumer PropTech benchmark globally. India equivalent would be 99acres + PropEquity combined.

positioning: Tech-powered real estate brokerage with 1% listing fee
description: Redfin combines a consumer portal (like Zillow) with its own salaried agents who charge lower commission (~1% vs standard 2.5–3%). Offers mortgage, title, and renovation services. Known for best-in-class map search and data accuracy. Listed on NASDAQ.
key_problem: Traditional US real estate commission (5–6%) is too high; buyers get poor data and delayed response from traditional agents.
revenue_model: Agent commission (1% listing), mortgage origination, partner referral fees. Revenue: ~$1B (FY2024)
differentiator: Own salaried agents + best listing data freshness (updated every 5 min from MLS)
weakness: US urban-centric; rural/land coverage thin. Struggling with profitability.
notes: Agent + data hybrid model. Illustrates how property data + services can be bundled.

positioning: UK's #1 property portal — 85% market share in online property search
description: Rightmove is the dominant UK property listings portal with 85% market share. Agents must list on Rightmove — making it an essential monopoly. Extremely profitable: 70%+ EBITDA margins. Listed on London Stock Exchange.
key_problem: UK home buyers and renters had no single reliable place to search all properties.
revenue_model: Agent membership fees (no performance/transaction fee) — agents pay ~£1,000–£2,000/month. Revenue: ~£340M (FY2024)
differentiator: Near-monopoly network effect. 70%+ EBITDA margins. Agents can't opt out.
weakness: No data/analytics product. No due diligence. Pure portal play.
notes: Example of portal network effect moat. Comparable India play would require 99acres to dominate similarly — hasn't happened yet due to 3-way competition.

positioning: Southeast Asia's leading property portal — Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, Vietnam
description: PropertyGuru dominates the SEA property search market across 4 countries. Offers listings, mortgage comparison, valuation tools (Guru-powered AVM), and developer analytics. Listed on NYSE after SPAC merger.
key_problem: Property search in SEA markets was fragmented, opaque, and agent-controlled. No central discovery platform.
revenue_model: Agent and developer listing fees, premium placement, mortgage referral. Revenue: ~$130M (FY2024)
differentiator: Market-leading position in 4 SEA countries + data/valuation products + developer tools
weakness: No India presence. Data products thinner than US peers.
notes: Closest global analogue to what 99acres/Housing.com should evolve into. Their data product 'FastKey' is a developer CRM that KYL could eventually compete with.

positioning: America's largest rural land marketplace — farms, ranches, hunting land, timber
description: LandWatch is the premier US marketplace for rural land — 8M+ acres listed at any time. Owned by CoStar Group. Covers farms, ranches, hunting properties, timberland, and vacant land. App on Play Store and App Store.
key_problem: Rural/agricultural land in the US has no consumer-friendly search portal — it was sold exclusively through rural land agents.
revenue_model: Agent/broker listing subscriptions, premium placement. Part of CoStar (NASDAQ: CSGP).
differentiator: Largest rural land listing database in the US. CoStar data infrastructure backing.
weakness: US rural-only. No due diligence or analytics product. Listing-only play.
notes: Most directly relevant global analogue for land-specific portal. LandWatch for India does not exist — that's the KYL opportunity on the portal side.

positioning: US land parcel data and boundary intelligence
description: Regrid is the leading US land parcel data provider — maintaining 150M+ parcel records with owner information, boundaries, zoning, and legal descriptions. Powers real estate, insurance, solar, telecom, and agriculture decisions.
key_problem: US parcel data is fragmented across 3,000+ county assessors. Regrid normalises it as a single queryable dataset.
revenue_model: API subscriptions ($500–$50,000/yr), bulk data licensing, enterprise deals
differentiator: Only nationwide US parcel dataset with consistent schema; API-first
weakness: US only. No applicability to India's fragmented land records.
notes: Best international analogue for what KYL aims to build for India. Regrid = KYL's north star product.

positioning: US property data platform for lenders, title companies, and investors
description: DataTree is First American's property data platform providing ownership, lien, mortgage, and title plant data across 99% of the US population. Used by banks, title insurers, and investors. First American is a Fortune 500 title insurance company.
key_problem: Title and lien research requires access to fragmented county records. DataTree aggregates 40+ years of title plant data.
revenue_model: Subscription SaaS, per-report fees, bulk data licensing
funding_stage: Part of First American Financial (NYSE: FAF, $6B revenue)
differentiator: First American's 40-year title plant; deepest lien and encumbrance history in the US
weakness: US-only. Enterprise pricing. No environmental or demand analytics.
notes: First American earns ~$6B from title insurance + data. Illustrates how large the data layer of land transactions can become.

positioning: The Bloomberg of commercial real estate — global property intelligence platform
description: CoStar provides the most comprehensive commercial real estate database in the world — 6M+ properties with sales comps, lease comps, availability, tenant data, and analytics. Owns LoopNet, Apartments.com, Homes.com, LandWatch, and STR (hotel data). Listed on NASDAQ.
key_problem: Commercial real estate has no public price discovery. CoStar acts as the data backbone for every CRE transaction.
revenue_model: Subscription SaaS ($500–$5,000/user/month). Advertising on LoopNet/Homes.com. Revenue: ~$2.5B (FY2024)
differentiator: Largest proprietary dataset (500+ field researchers). Network effects.
weakness: Extremely expensive. US/Europe focused. Not land/plot specific.
notes: Long-term aspiration for a mature Indian RE data platform. Has announced India expansion intentions.

positioning: Satellite-based land intelligence — change detection, land use classification
description: Orbital Insight uses satellite imagery and ML to track physical changes on land — construction progress, agricultural land-use change, deforestation, infrastructure. Serves commodity traders, hedge funds, insurance companies, and governments. Acquired by Maxar Technologies.
key_problem: Physical asset monitoring at scale is impossible with human inspection. Satellite data + AI enables continuous monitoring of millions of parcels.
revenue_model: Enterprise SaaS contracts ($100K–$10M/yr). Government defense contracts via Maxar.
differentiator: Maxar's satellite constellation (30cm resolution); proven AI models for change detection
weakness: Expensive ($100K+ contracts). No India-specific title/legal layer. Not consumer-accessible.
notes: Represents the geospatial angle that could augment KYL's physical land attribute data.
